prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: atom‐condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilic attack), f⁻ (nucleophilic attack), f⁰ (radical attack), and the dual descriptor for pyridine; headline value reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui index f⁺.
Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density-fitting (PySCF).
Geometry provenance: single-point charges on the user-supplied geometry from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz; no geometry optimization was performed (DFT//input-xyz).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
Electronic state: neutral molecule charge 0, multiplicity 1. Ionized states: cation (+1, doublet) and anion (–1, doublet) used for the finite-difference definition of the Fukui functions.
Software: PySCF backend (version as bundled with chemkit, not explicitly reported).
Convergence & warnings: SCF cycles for the N, N+1, and N–1 states all converged; integrity.status = ok, trustworthy = true. Warnings returned by the engine are reproduced verbatim below.

Key numerical results (Mulliken scheme)
Most electrophilic site: atom 1 (N), f⁺ = 0.48422572
Most nucleophilic site: atom 4 (C),  f⁻ = 0.11826260
Sum over atoms: Σf⁺ = 1.0000 Σf⁻ = 1.0000 (charge conserved)
Dual descriptor (f⁺ – f⁻) indicates N (atom 1) is the strongest electrophilic center; several hydrogens are most nucleophilically disfavored (negative dual descriptor).

Live run details
Calculation was executed with the command:
/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp2e44t8ow/live.json
The PySCF/stdout log was streamed live to the caller’s working directory (path shown at launch); see that .out file for full SCF histories. Parsed results are in /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp2e44t8ow/live.json.
